feedback.text: As a video game developer, I strongly support the goals of the Digital Fairness Act. The points raised especially around dark patterns, addictive design, and manipulative monetization strategies are issues I've seen firsthand in the industry. I believe game design should prioritize player experience and meaningful engagement, not exploit psychological vulnerabilities through gambling-like features, false urgency, or pressure to spend. The rise of these practices in live-service games often undermines the actual quality and creativity of the game itself. I also appreciate the focus on transparency in subscriptions and the accountability of social media influencers. Consumers deserve fair, honest digital environments that respect their time, attention, and well-being. Personally, I cannot speak against these practices in public for fear of financial persecution/retribution, therefore I thank you for taking a stand on these issues and bringing more attention to this very controversial and taboo subject within the video game industry. Me and my associate developers fully support you on this endeavor and are following this act with interest and hope. I hope this Act moves forward and helps steer the digital industry especially games toward more ethical, player-respecting practices. Sincerely, Aspiring and concerned video game developer from Finland.
